meaning: n.公开侮辱,轻蔑vt.公开侮辱,冒犯,面对
examples: What a Westerner considers an honest look in the eye, the Oriental takes as a lack of respect and a personal affront.
The play was considered an affront to public morals.
Life is too short to turn every personal affront into a battle.
Never really having been challenged, when the challenge finally comes, it seems an affront.
"It's an affront to us, it makes us wonder what's going on, "he says.
